text: Iron deficiency affects approximately 15-35% of female athlete cohorts, making it the most common mineral deficiency in this population.
Iron deficiency affects approximately 5-11% of male athlete cohorts, a lower but still clinically significant prevalence compared to females.
Exercise-induced increases in hepcidin, the master iron regulatory hormone, contribute to altered iron metabolism in athletes and may impair iron absorption.
Iron is critical for oxygen transport and energy metabolism, making iron status a direct factor in endurance sport performance.
Sex hormones influence iron regulation in athletes, contributing to the higher prevalence of iron deficiency in female compared to male athletes.

Training load, dietary macronutrient manipulation, and environmental stress such as altitude-induced hypoxia all affect an athlete's iron status and should be considered in iron management strategies.
